mechanism_narrative: A sustained upward trend in real retail sales, proxied by RSAFS, signals robust consumer demand that translates into higher corporate revenues and earnings, driving positive S&P 500 forward 20-day returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are not positive in more than 55% of months when RSAFS is increasing versus decreasing, or the Rank IC between RSAFS changes and forward returns is below 0.03 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 5%, restrictive monetary policy raises the cost of capital and slows economic activity, leading to negative S&P 500 forward 60-day returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when FEDFUNDS exceeds 5%, or the mean return conditional on the trigger is not significantly below zero (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in UNRATE and decreases in PAYEMS signal a weakening labor market that erodes aggregate income and consumer confidence, producing negative S&P 500 forward 20-day returns. The joint occurrence is a stronger signal than either series alone.
failure_condition: The joint signal does not produce negative forward returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the interaction term adds no incremental explanatory power over individual series in a logistic regression (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle, an accelerating CPIAUCSL inflation reading causes the Federal Reserve to maintain or increase the federal funds rate, raising discount rates and depressing equity valuations, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward 5-day returns.
failure_condition: In identified tightening cycles, positive S&P 500 forward 5-day returns occur in more than 50% of months with accelerating CPIAUCSL, or the mean conditional return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production (INDPRO) signals contracting manufacturing output and weaker corporate earnings in the industrial and materials sectors, leading to negative S&P 500 forward 20-day returns.
failure_condition: Negative forward returns occur in fewer than 55% of months when INDPRO declines, or the mean return conditional on the trigger is not significantly below zero (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained drop in housing starts (HOUST) below 1.9 million units signals weakening residential investment and impending slowdown in construction and consumer durable demand, generating negative S&P 500 forward 60-day returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances when HOUST is below 1900, or the mean conditional return is not significantly below zero (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is above 5% and retail sales growth is simultaneously decelerating, the combination of tight monetary policy and weakening consumer demand produces sharply negative S&P 500 forward 20-day returns.
failure_condition: The joint trigger does not produce negative forward returns in more than 60% of instances, or a logistic regression shows no significant interaction effect (p > 0.05) beyond the main effects of FEDFUNDS and RSAFS.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real GDP (GDPC1) reflects the aggregate output of the economy; positive GDP growth surprises lead to upward revisions in earnings expectations and drive positive S&P 500 forward 60-day returns.
failure_condition: Positive GDP growth quarters do not coincide with positive S&P 500 forward 60-day returns in more than 55% of cases, or the Rank IC between GDPC1 growth and forward returns is below 0.03 over a 5-year rolling window.
